meta.title: X200 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Jaguar S-Type X200 engine — 3.0 V6, 4.2 V8, S-Type R, 2.7 diesel. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and Ford-era reliability. Get free quotes from 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar S-Type engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X200 engine typically costs £2,200–£9,000 supply only, depending on engine — the common 3.0 V6 sits at £2,500–£4,500, while the supercharged S-Type R runs £5,000–£9,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,170. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which S-Type X200 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.0-litre AJ-V6 is the most dependable — the most common S-Type engine, with a smooth, reliable character and well-understood maintenance needs. The 4.2-litre V8 (post-2002) is also highly reliable and improved over the earlier 4.0 unit.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the S-Type X200?

acceptedAnswer.text: Thermostat housing and coolant leaks on the V6 petrols, timing chain tensioner wear on the 4.0 V8, and injector/EGR/turbo issues on the 2.7 diesel. The 4.2 V8 is improved; the supercharged S-Type R has additional supercharger maintenance costs.

mainEntity.name: Is the S-Type X200 reliable overall?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the S-Type is generally considered one of Jaguar's more dependable Ford-era models. The 3.0 V6 is the sweet spot; the 4.2 V8 is refined and reliable; the 2.7 diesel is more complex but manageable with specialist care. The 4.0 V8 (1999–2002) needs chain attention.

mainEntity.name: Which S-Type X200 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but a 4.0 V8 without documented timing chain history deserves a thorough inspection. A 2.7 diesel without injector or EGR service history is also a potential liability. The 3.0 V6 is the safest bet.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my S-Type X200 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss or overheating (V6 thermostat housing), cold-start rattle (V8 timing chain), or rough running and smoke (2.7 diesel injectors/EGR) are key warnings. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an S-Type X200 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £4,000+ S-Type, a £2,800 3.0 V6 recon is 70% of the car's value — approaching our 60% Jaguar threshold, assess carefully. Against a tired £2,500 example, the same recon is 112% — walk away. Value the car first.

mainEntity.name: How long do S-Type X200 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.0 V6 can comfortably exceed 150,000 miles with regular servicing. The 4.2 V8 is similarly durable. The 4.0 V8's longevity depends on chain care — addressed proactively, it can reach 150,000+ miles. The 2.7 diesel is complex but capable of 150,000+ miles with rigorous maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the S-Type R and how does it differ?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S-Type R (2002–2008) is the performance flagship — powered by a supercharged 4.2-litre V8 producing 396–405 PS. It features uprated suspension, larger brakes and unique styling. It's the most expensive S-Type to maintain but also the most rewarding.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my S-Type X200 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Check the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, or the engine block — 3.0 V6 engines are marked AJ-V6 or FB, 4.2 V8 as AJ8FT, 4.2 supercharged as AJ8FT SC, 2.7 diesel as AJD or AJTDV6. Tell us your variant and registration year and we'll identify it — 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Jaguar S-Type X200 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the S-Type X200 — from the 3.0-litre AJ-V6 to the 4.0-litre V8, the supercharged 4.2 S-Type R, and the PSA-developed 2.7-litre diesel. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and Jaguar's Ford-era revival. Backed by 70+ real UK X200 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Jaguar S-Type X200 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the S-Type X200 specifically — from the launch 3.0 V6 and 4.0 V8 to the later 2.5 V6, 4.2 V8, supercharged S-Type R and the 2.7 diesel.

overview.intro: The S-Type (project code X200) marked Jaguar's return to the mid-size executive sector, launched at the 1998 British International Motor Show and entering UK sales in 1999. Built at Castle Bromwich, it was the first all-new saloon introduced after Ford's 1989 takeover. The engine range launched with Jaguar's AJ-V6 (3.0 litres) and AJ-V8 (4.0 litres). A 2002 revision brought a 2.5-litre V6 and replaced the 4.0 with a 4.2-litre V8; the supercharged 4.2 S-Type R followed. A 2004 facelift added a 2.7-litre diesel developed with PSA, and a final 2007 revision brought further updates before the XF replaced it in 2008.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Ford DEW98 (shared with Lincoln LS) • Predecessor: N/A (return to segment) • Successor: Jaguar XF (X250) • Sibling body styles in this generation: N/A — X200 is the sole body style.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The S-Type X200 generated 70 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 3.0 V6 and 2.7 diesel as these Ford-era Jaguars enter affordable-classic territory.

items.quote: The 3.0-litre AJ-V6 is the most common S-Type engine and the one to trust — smooth, reliable and well-supported. It's the engine that defined the X200's driving character.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the classic S-Type experience without V8 complexity.

items.quote: The 2.7 diesel is a PSA-Ford joint venture engine — the same unit used in Land Rover and Peugeot/Citroën products. Known for injector, EGR and turbo issues; it's the most complex engine in the X200 range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel economy and are prepared for specialist maintenance.

items.quote: Supercharger rebuilds, cooling system failures and timing chain work on the 4.2 SC can run into thousands. This is the most expensive S-Type to maintain — but also the most rewarding.
items.whoItsFor: enthusiasts who want 400bhp and are ready for the bills that come with it.

items.quote: The most common X200 engine by far, with cheap parts, nationwide specialist coverage, and a well-understood failure profile. A £2,500–£4,500 recon makes financial sense against a £1,500–£5,500 vehicle.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most cost-effective S-Type ownership.

items.quote: The 2.5 V6 is rarer than the 3.0 but offers similar reliability with slightly better economy. It's often overlooked by buyers chasing the bigger engines — but it's a genuinely solid choice.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the S-Type experience with lower running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Solid Choice — the most common and reliable S-Type
Watch Closely — early V8s need chain attention
Best V8 — more refined and reliable than the 4.0
Most Expensive — fast, but costly to maintain

economicsRule.title: The X200 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,500–£4,500 3.0 V6 recon against a £1,500–£5,500 S-Type sits at 45–300% of the car's value. On a sub-£2,500 high-mileage example, that brushes our 60% Jaguar threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £4,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 63–100% — assess carefully. Value the car first.

problems.title: 3.0/2.5 V6 Thermostat Housing & Coolant Leaks
problems.rootCause: The plastic thermostat housing can crack with age and heat cycling; coolant leaks are the most common V6 failure. The engine is otherwise robust — this is the main weak point.
problems.recommendation: On any high-mileage V6 S-Type, inspect the thermostat housing for cracks and check for coolant loss. A £300–£700 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,500+ replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare 3.0 V6 engine prices for your S-Type →

problems.title: 4.0 V8 Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.rootCause: Early AJ-V8 engines suffer from timing chain tensioner wear — a well-documented issue on the 4.0-litre unit. Rattle on startup is the classic early warning. The 4.2-litre that replaced it in 2002 is improved.
problems.recommendation: If your 4.0 V8 S-Type has no documented chain history, treat it as a priority inspection — £800–£1,600 now is far cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows a failure. Cold-start rattle is the warning sign to watch for.

cta.label: Compare 4.0 V8 engine prices for your S-Type →

problems.title: 2.7 Diesel Injector & EGR Failure
problems.rootCause: The 2.7-litre PSA-Ford diesel (AJD/AJTDV6) is known for injector failure, EGR clogging and turbocharger issues. It's a complex engine that requires specialist attention — the same unit used in Land Rover and Peugeot/Citroën products.
problems.recommendation: The 2.7 diesel is the most complex engine in the X200 range — regular servicing and early intervention are essential. At the first sign of rough running or smoke, get it inspected by a specialist who knows this PSA-Ford unit.

cta.label: Compare 2.7 D engine prices for your S-Type →

replacementCosts.h2: X200 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Engine Page baseline — no dedicated Engine Pages exist for these families yet, so baseline figures are drawn from the EM_Proprietary_Data Table A. Labour hours estimated at 10–14 hours for V6 and diesel engines, 12–16 for V8s, and 14–18 for the supercharged V8 due to additional complexity.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are S-Type X200 only. The S-Type R is the supercharged 4.2-litre V8 performance flagship. The 4.0 V8 was produced 1999–2002; the 4.2 V8 replaced it from 2002. The 2.5 V6 was introduced in 2002. The 2.7 diesel joined in 2004.
